messages.text: Basket 1 is a woven wicker basket shaped like a duck: oval body with a lid, and a duck head on the right side (little beak and a blue eye).
Basket 2: a deep, rounded bucket-shaped brown wicker basket with one thick very dark (almost black) arched handle; rim has a banded look with a lighter strip just under the top.
Basket 3: rectangular picnic basket with a tall center handle, and a hinged lid that’s open/flipped up to the right side.
Is it the one with the lid already flipped open to the right (not the fully closed lidded box)?
Yes—basket 3 is the rectangular picnic basket with the tall center handle and the lid already flipped open to the right (not the fully closed one).
Basket 4: the light tan/cream oval “boat-shaped” wicker basket with one arched handle that has white-wrapped sections (and small reddish bands where it attaches).
Basket 5: a squat rectangular wicker basket with a very thick twisted-rope handle; tight square weave and a darker band around the middle.
Is it the low, rectangular basket with a chunky braided handle (not the taller greenish rectangular one)?
Yes—pick the low/squat rectangular basket with the chunky braided/twisted handle (not the taller greenish rectangular one).
Basket 6: the round lidded wicker basket shaped like a CAT face—ears on top, painted whiskers/nose, little feet.
Basket 7: rectangular basket with one arched handle; body is green/teal woven with vertical striping, and it has a thick braided warm-brown rim/top edge.
Basket 8: oval basket with a thick dark (black/charcoal) arched handle; body is dark gray wicker with a lighter tan band around the bottom and a pale rim at the top.
Basket 9: tall dark-brown cylindrical wicker basket, open top, with TWO small loop handles on the rim (no big arched handle).
Basket 10: a shallow, wide round tray-like wicker basket with one arched handle over the top; the base shows a clear spiral/coil weave (light tan with a bit of reddish-brown).
Basket 11: deep round tub/bucket basket with TWO small loop handles on the sides (no arch handle), thick rim, diagonal chevron weave—darker brown near the top fading to lighter tan below.
Basket 12: light tan wicker basket with one arched handle; rounded-rectangle shape with a slightly pinched/waisted top opening and open lattice sides (no lid).
Is it the pale basket with a narrow waist near the rim (not the round shallow tray and not the two-tone banded one)?
Yes—that’s Basket 12: the pale tan handled basket with the narrow “waist” right near the rim (open lattice sides), not the shallow round tray and not the green two-tone banded one.

ai_director_reasoning.partner_comment: My partner consistently placed the correct basket quickly and kept the rounds moving. They asked good clarifying questions when a description could match multiple options (e.g., lid open vs closed, handle color/thickness, tray vs tote, loop handles vs arched handle), which reduced ambiguity. There were a couple moments of brief confusion in Round 3 (rechecking Basket 5), but they recovered immediately once I clarified. Overall they were capable, communicative, and our coordination got smoother over time.

ai_matcher_reasoning.partner_comment: Overall my partner gave clear, distinctive visual descriptions (colors, handle types, lids/animal shapes, bands) that were usually enough to identify the right basket quickly. They responded well to clarifying questions (e.g., handle color, lid vs no lid, tray vs tote) and corrected ambiguity when I flagged potential confusions. A few moments in round 3 had some repetition/misalnumbering (e.g., re-stating “pos5” while I was asking for basket 6/8), which briefly slowed things down, but the coordination still improved across rounds and we finished accurately.
